$comment: Beats for the ~1.5-min Stardew Clawd intro video. Feature-focused tour — no technique bragging. Each beat: TTS text + an action key the orchestrator runs WHILE the audio plays. A global ambient ticker fires random tool activity on all workers throughout, so the office is never visually still.
instructions: Warm, conversational tech-doc voice. Even pace. Slight pauses at periods.

beats.text: Claude Code sessions are invisible. You start one in a terminal, and you wait. Stardew Clawd turns every running session into a pixel-art worker, sitting at a desk in a cozy office.

When Claude reads a file, the worker reads. When it edits code, they type. When it runs a shell command, they bash on the keyboard. The whole office hums along with what your sessions are actually doing.
When Claude needs your input — a question, or a plan to approve — a sign pops up over the worker's head. Click them to answer right there.
Your answer flows back to Claude and the session continues. No switching to the terminal, no losing the thread.
Click any worker to open their transcript. Long bursts of tool calls fold into a single chip — twelve reads collapse to one row you can expand on demand.
Hire more workers and the office grows. Multiple rooms, drag to pan the camera, slider to zoom out and see everyone at once.
Don't like the cozy farm look? Swap scenes. Modern office, school, lab, construction site — same workers, same desks, totally different vibe.
And up in the corner, a live readout of your Claude Code quota — five-hour and weekly bars — so you always know how much runway you have left.
That's Stardew Clawd. Pixel-art workers for your Claude Code sessions. It's open source — link in the description.
